person.name: Constanze Roedig
person.bio: Key Researcher & Lecturer, Public Speaker, Creator of the Software Bill of Behavior (SBoB) and maintainer of the Kubernetes Storm Center for cloudnative intelligence Backstory: Living intentionally is hard when routine takes control. I grew up wanting to be a scientist, studying Biochemistry, starting med school, and ultimately earning a PhD in General Relativity to explore how humans perceive 'reality.' When US funding cuts ended that path, I realized academia left me without agency.In the private sector, I discovered the value of risk. The Cloud Native paradigm let me experiment, fail fast, and try again without fear of lasting damage. In positive-sum collaborations, I saw how my algorithms could shape real-world outcomes, and how taking calculated risks created visible change for people and systems alike. This freedom to test ideas with relatively low cost revealed a new kind of agency, an immediacy and measurable connection to the meaning of work.My vision is to empower others to leverage cloud native technology with respect for the human element, security, and risk. Because having agency means creating reality for our society, for each one of us, one epsilon at a time.

person.bio: Zohaib UL-Hassan is a dedicated Cloud Solutions Architect and DevOps Engineer with over 15 years of experience in DevOps, automation, solution architecture, and cloud-native technologies. He specializes in designing scalable, resilient systems that empower innovation and operational excellence. As a passionate Kubernetes enthusiast, he is deeply engaged with container orchestration and cloud-native ecosystems. Additionally, he is passionate about AI and MLOps, actively exploring ways to integrate machine learning workflows into cloud infrastructure to enhance automation, optimize performance, and deliver intelligent, data-driven solutions.

person.name: Andrii Berezniuk
person.bio: Andrii Berezniuk is a DevOps engineer with 10+ years of experience in the industry. He specializes in the development and transformation of cloud-native applications, DevSecOps, and CI/CD implementations, with the focus on scalable and secure solutions to complex business problems. Connect with Andrii on LinkedIn to learn more about his work and insights.

person.name: Jagadeesan Arumugam
person.bio: Hi, I am Jaga, Solution Arichitect and having 20+ Years of experience in IT, specialised in Virtualization. I am also certified GCP PCA.

person.name: Romain Boulanger
person.bio: Eternally passionate and deeply curious, I’ve always enjoyed learning on my own, particularly anything to do with automation, containerisation and IT security.  I’m also a big fan of open-source technologies, which I experiment with on a daily basis as I set up my own infrastructure to host my personal blog: blog.filador.ch. With a rate of one article a month, I love sharing what I discover!  Finally, I’ve had the opportunity to work with several cloud providers: Google Cloud, AWS, Azure and even AliCloud. If you ask me the question, “Which one do you prefer?” I’d answer that it depends on your use case!  Since 2024, I’ve been an Ambassador for the Silicon Chalet. This Meetup brings together a community of over 1,600 members from Switzerland and France! My role is to promote tools and approaches close to my heart, either by organising events or by presenting topics.

person.name: Sébastien Davoult
person.bio: Passionate about Virtualization, Containerization and Observability technologies, I have made it my job. Today, I accompany our customers in their #MoveToCloud transformation mainly on OnPremise environments by maximizing the use of OpenSource products. Much more than just products, OpenSource is for me a philosophy. I love to share and transmit on the subjects which impassion me, I do it with students, conferences, and also by publishing specialized blogs posts.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am very happy and excited to have joined this program. It will allow me to promote the Cloud Native more widely in France. It's a subject that is still unknown in some companies and the specialized content is often not translated from English. My goal is to promote the power of the Cloud Native through French articles, Meetups, and conferences.

person.name: Tim Berry
person.bio: I've been working in tech for over 25 years as an engineer, consultant and leader. I'm old enough to remember what we had to put up with before Kubernetes, so I'm a huge fan! These days I help organisations to be successful with technical training and enablement, and I write about cloud engineering, python and occasionally game development at https://timberry.dev

person.name: Amro Elamin
person.bio: A cloud architect that’s enthusiastic about container orchestration and open source cloud-native technologies.

person.name: Bosch Luckas
person.bio: I’m a Site Reliability Engineer based in New York, passionate about Kubernetes, cloud-native infrastructure, everything Linux-related, and open source. I also host La Tangente, a French tech podcast where I interview engineers, entrepreneurs, and open source contributors.
person.location: New york, New york, United States

person.name: Karthik Kota
person.bio: I am a tech enthusiast, finding the perfect balance between high-speed innovation and the strategic learning. My passion shows my drive to constantly evolve and I’ve embraced the ‘Believe’ mantra —it adds a layer of authentic motivation that pairs perfectly with my sharp, tech-focused mind.

person.name: David Lautie
person.bio: For me, Kubernetes embodies the future of cloud and modern infrastructure. It’s a technology that combines robustness, agility, and openness. Seeing thousands of passionate people collaborate within the CNCF and the Open Source ecosystem to build this living platform reminds me why I chose to work in this field: to learn, to share, and to innovate together.

person.name: Eric Ng
person.bio: Senior Business Architect in VMware. Over 20 years in IT/Networking. Specialized in DCN, DC Security and Modern Application.

person.name: Wolfram Gerret Apelt
person.bio: Gerret is a technology enthusiast with over 20 years of experience in IT operations consulting. He got into programming as a teenager, built his first OpenStack private cloud in 2015 and thinks of documentation as an art-form. He thrives in architectural roles that require vision and challenge his deep technical expertise. He is employed as a Senior Operations Engineer with IS Nordic A/S where he designs, implements and operates strong Kubernetes clusters both on-premise and in the cloud, for clients as well as as in-house workloads.

person.name: Naresh Duddu
person.bio:
Naresh holds the position of Associate Vice President and Global Head of Modernization Practice at Infosys. In his role, he oversees the development of intellectual property (IP), collaboration with modernization partners, and introduction of new services within the domain of application modernization. The practice led by Naresh takes a prominent role in spearheading application transformation initiatives across diverse industries and service lines. It places particular emphasis on four fundamental pillars of application modernization: Open Source, Agile/DevOps, Legacy modernization, and Cloud.

Over the course of his 29-year career, Naresh has occupied various roles that have encompassed IT services delivery, program management, enterprise architecture, as well as incubating and fostering multiple specialized groups.

Naresh holds a B Tech in Metallurgical Engineering from Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi, India.

person.name: Stephan Berger

In recent years, he has focused on cloud-native and Kubernetes platforms, specializing in CNCF technologies with a strong emphasis on GitOps practices and automation. Passionate about open-source and continuous learning, he regularly attends KubeCon and other cloud-native community events to exchange ideas and stay at the forefront of the CNCF ecosystem.

person.name: Yassine Gazzah
person.bio: As a technical specialist and consultant, I partner with clients to deliver complex projects using Kubernetes, OpenShift, Ansible, and a broad range of cloud-native technologies. My work focuses on building practical, scalable solutions that solve real-world infrastructure challenges through direct project delivery and architecture design. Beyond consulting, I am deeply committed to sharing knowledge as both a Linux Foundation Authorized Instructor and a Red Hat Certified Architect and Instructor. This allows me to bridge the gap between high-level theory and hands-on implementation, helping engineers and organizations master the tools necessary for modern cloud environments.

person.name: Maksim Burakouski
person.bio: I am a DevOps/SRE engineer passionate about building reliable, scalable, and secure infrastructure, with a strong focus on Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. I enjoy improving CI/CD pipelines, automating operational workflows, and reducing manual toil so teams can deliver software faster and more efficiently.

person.name: Vishal Kohli
person.bio: Vishal has 22 years of proven experience in enterprise scale project spanning across Design, Development, Transformation and Operations & has expertise in Cloud Migration, Infrastructure setup (IAC), Containerization/Container Orchestration and DevSecOps along with strong interpersonal skills

person.name: Idris Wishiwala
person.bio: I’m a DevSecOps engineer and CNCF enthusiast who spends an unhealthy amount of time breaking and fixing Kubernetes clusters for fun. I specialize in container security, cloud-native automation, and building clusters that refuse to die even when developers try their hardest. When I’m not tinkering with CKA/CKS-style lab environments, I write concise technical posts on real-world DevOps problems and how to avoid shooting yourself in the foot with YAML

person.name: Janelle Dougherty
person.bio: I've been a devops engineer for the last seven years. I love building, automating, and troubleshooting infrastructure across diverse environments. I'm passionate about all things kubernetes and cloud-native. And, absolutely love tinkering with things even though this usually means they get broken.

person.name: Rahul Kumar

I primarily use CNCF open source technologies to develop and design robust platforms and solutions for production. For example, I created a multiregional enterprise MLOps platform that is used daily by thousands of data scientists and involves scaling Spark on Kubernetes. Additionally, I guide clients on best practices in the Kubernetes stack.

person.name: Wajhat Syed
person.bio: Currently working at Dell Technologies, handling Private On-Premise Kubernetes clusters at Samsung. I started my IT career working with Kubernetes a few years ago, and it quickly became a key part of my skillset. Over time, I've gained extensive experience in container orchestration, monitoring multiple Kubernetes clusters, and troubleshooting Kubernetes system has been central to my growth, helping me build more efficient and resilient infrastructures.

person.name: Claudio Domingos
person.bio: With over 15 years in IT, I specialize in aligning architecture, operations, and security with industry best practices. My focus is on automation, cloud infrastructure, and DevSecOps to help clients build secure, efficient, and reliable systems. I value clear communication, practical solutions, and continuous improvement. Always open to sharing ideas and learning from others — let’s connect!

person.name: Joe Kwong
person.bio: Joe is a Lead Solution Architect at VMware by Broadcom. Previously worked for Amazon Web Services as a Partner Solution Architect and Google Cloud Partner as Lead Customer Engineer. He help customer how to harness latest platform technology to simply their K8s operation and deliver value to customer fast.

person.name: Ryunosuke Suzuki
person.bio: Engineer at Google Cloud.  To stay on the cutting edge, I'm constantly learning and honing my skills. Lately, I've taken a strong interest in AI/ML and Big Data, particularly technologies like Kueue and KubeFlow. I'm also passionate about giving back to the community and helping fellow engineers grow, believing that this helps foster a more adaptable and expansive cloud-native ecosystem.  One of the maintainers of [KHI](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/khi), a Google Cloud open-source project.

person.name: Shabbir Saifee
person.bio: I am a Senior SRE at McKesson, specializing in Kubernetes and CNCF technologies.  I create Kubernetes and CNCF tutorials on my YouTube channel 'CookNCode'.

person.name: Tom Chrulski
person.bio: I'm an experienced IT professional with a passion for secure, resilient systems and continuous improvement. I enjoy solving complex infrastructure challenges, mentoring others, and driving positive change across organizations. Lifelong learning is at the heart of how I work and lead. Above all, I love spending time with my two sons—whether we’re playing, exploring, or just being silly together. When I get a moment to myself, you’ll find me at the piano, over a game of chess, or solving Hanayama puzzles. BTW. I use NixOS—because reproducibility matters, in systems and in life.

person.name: Jayesh Pal
person.bio: I am a seasoned Cloud Architect with over 15 years of experience in designing and implementing robust cloud architectures across industries such as airlines, telecom, and healthcare. Skilled in Azure, Kubernetes, DevOps, and cloud security.

person.name: Rohit Ghumare
person.bio: Rohit Ghumare is a recognized Google Developer Expert in AI and Cloud, Docker Captain, Platform Ambassador, CNCF Marketing Chair 2025 a testament to his adeptness in contemporary cloud solutions. Contributed to Platform Engineer Associate exam and Certified Backstage Exam. He is the driving force behind several community-centric initiatives, orchestrating events as the organizer of KCD UK, Cloud Native Westminster London, DevOpsDays Bengaluru, KCD Mumbai, Google Cloud Mumbai, and the Cloud Native Thane community group. Through these platforms, he not only showcases his profound expertise but also fosters a collaborative ecosystem for knowledge exchange. A prolific writer, Rohit has authored a plethora of blogs and insightful pieces on CNCF projects, including, but not limited to, Backstage, Istio, Kubernetes, Cilium, Envoy, Prometheus, and Kiali. This extensive coverage underscores his profound understanding and engagement with cutting-edge cloud-native technologies. Besides penning down his knowledge, Rohit is dedicated to sharing his insights through the "Cloud Native Talks" podcast, which he hosts, and through global conferences and local meetups where he's presented multiple talks. He has also been a program committee member for Kubecon co-located events. Links: devopsguys.inWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: Being appointed as an ambassador presents a remarkable opportunity to further extend the outreach of my engagement within the tech community, particularly in the realms of DevOps and Cloud Native technologies. It will help me to give back to the community by sharing insights from experts located all over the world.

person.name: Tiffany Jernigan
person.bio: Tiffany is a CNCF Ambassador, seasoned technologist and content creator in the Cloud Native space. She most recently was a senior developer advocate at VMware. She also formerly worked as a software developer and developer advocate at Amazon, Docker, and Intel. Prior to that, she graduated from Georgia Tech with a degree in electrical engineering. You can find her at tiffanyfay.dev (and for Bluesky).

person.name: Piotr Prusaczyk

person.name: Shrikumar Udupa

person.name: Thomas Balbier
person.bio: I'm a passionate DevOps Engineer with a strong focus on Kubernetes and modern cloud-native workflows. person.name: Ashish Kumar Matapathi

person.name: Bijay Pathak

person.name: Cesar Yoab Villarreal Guzman

person.name: Ivan Sukhanov
person.bio: I'm a Cloud and DevOps Engineer with expertise in managing cloud infrastructure and Kubernetes environments with a strong focus on automation and DevOps practices.

person.name: Luca Cavallin
person.bio: Senior Software Engineer at GitHub Actions, passionate about developer and infrastructure tools, distributed systems, systems- and network-programming, sharing knowledge. person.name: Sebastian Francisco Colomar Bauza

In addition to his consulting work, Sebastian delivers training on Linux, Docker and Kubernetes.

person.name: Taylor Thomas

He is an org maintainer for the wasmCloud project and a participant in the CNCF Wasm Working Group. His work at Intel, Nike, and Microsoft spanned various containers and Kubernetes platforms as well as WebAssembly platforms.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am extremely excited to help the Cloud Native community grow, especially with Wasm. I have been involved in the CNCF for years and am happy to be giving back to a community that got me deeply involved in open source.

person.name: Mauricio Fernandes

He currently leads automation-first and AI-ready network initiatives at Marriott, applying Infrastructure as Code, SRE practices, and data-driven operations to improve reliability and scale. Previously, he served as a Senior Solutions Architect at AWS, where he partnered with customers on cloud-native and AI/ML architectures. He is passionate about using automation and AI to transform infrastructure into resilient, self-service platforms.

person.name: Franck Coulibaly

I build and secure scalable platforms on the cloud, design service-to-service communication with service mesh, and ensure full observability through monitoring and tracing — while exploring how cloud, monolithic, containerized, and mobile technologies can work together to build reliable solutions.

person.name: Luis Alfonzo Rivas Marquez

Outside of work, I run a multi-cluster across multiple locations to implement high-availability patterns and operate my own services end-to-end.

person.name: Paula Kennedy

She is passionate about community, diversity and inclusion, enjoys speaking at events like KubeCon and also cohosted the first Platform Engineering colocated day at KubeCon Paris. In her spare time, Paula enjoys walking her dog and attempting to run half-marathons.

person.name: Yonathan Cedillos
person.bio: Senior DevOps Engineer with expertise configuring Flux and Argo and managing Cloud Infrastructure and Kubernetes cluster on AWS, GCP and Azure and recently Rancher for on-premise clusters. I also have experience configuring Prometheus and Grafana Stack for monitoring and observability.

person.name: Waleed Mohamed Kotyb

I architect cloud-native solutions using CNCF technologies including Kubernetes (RKE2, Rancher), Prometheus, Grafana, Argo CD, MetalLB, and advanced observability platforms, alongside enterprise platforms like OpenShift. As a certified Kubestronaut , I've led complex infrastructure transformations spanning bare-metal HPC clusters, NVIDIA DGX systems, and multi-cloud environments. I'm passionate about automation, Infrastructure-as-Code, and building resilient, scalable systems that empower teams to innovate faster.

person.name: Santosh Nukala
person.bio: Eagerly pursues knowledge in the ever-evolving field of CNCF technologies ,driven by deep-seated curiosity to innovate and optimize cloud and container security practices.

person.name: Melcolm Lee
person.bio: I am a Platform Engineer focusing on building and maintaining internal development  platforms in air-gapped environments with expertise in GitLab CI/CD, container security, Kubernetes security & AWS architecture.I design and implement secure, scalable, and resilient infrastructure, ensuring seamless automation and compliance in highly controlled environments while enhancing developer experience by improving automation and optimizing CI/CD pipelines within my organization.

person.name: Nick Jones
person.bio: Serial meetup organiser, including Cloud Native and Kubernetes Edinburgh, CNK Manchester, as well as larger events such as Kubernetes Community Days UK. Currently employed as Head of Engineering at Nscale, helping to build a next-generation managed Kubernetes service tailored for AI workloads. Passionate about new technology, but simultaneously romantic about the old with a penchant for decrepit Silicon Graphics and Sun Microsystems hardware. Sporadically blogs at https://dischord.orgWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: As a CNCF Ambassador, I'm really excited about formalising the ability to build bridges across the CNCF ecosystem, helping communities collaborate and support each other in their Cloud Native journey. I look forward to being able to leverage the resources and support provided by the CNCF to bring the best to the community events that I'm a part of.

person.name: Thomas Vaillant
person.bio: I’m a DevOps consultant with 10+ years helping teams ship reliable, cloud‑native software in high‑scale and regulated environments. I started as a full‑stack developer before specializing in DevOps, so I naturally bridge app code, infrastructure, and developer experience. Day to day you’ll find me on Kubernetes, Terraform, CI/CD, and observability; and I’m happy to jump into Go or TypeScript when it helps. I’ve led SRE teams and migrations from legacy to the cloud with a focus on security and pragmatic business impact. I love contributing to open source and leaving teams more autonomous than when I arrived.

person.name: Vinod Kumar

Currently serving as Principal Engineer (AWS) at Arcesium, managing data engineering, cloud infrastructure, SDLC processes, and ML models using AWS Bedrock. Previously led cloud migrations and cost-saving initiatives at a SaaS startup by transitioning from Salesforce to AWS. Holds triple AWS certifications, plus credentials in Google Cloud, Azure, and Kubernetes, with a strong commitment to continuous learning. Active contributor to CNCF and Terraform open-source projects, and shares knowledge through blogs on cloud-native technologies.

person.name: Gerrit Tamboer

I’m passionate about sharing insights and best practices in the CNCF ecosystem, actively engaging in the community to promote Kubernetes and edge computing.

person.name: Jörg Uhlig

My core expertise includes Kubernetes, OpenShift, ArgoCD, and the Grafana observability stack.  I’m passionate about building scalable, reliable, and maintainable platform solutions that create real value for customers and contribute positively to the global society. Whether by improving digital healthcare, advancing sustainable infrastructure, or contributing to open-source initiatives, I believe engineers have a responsibility to help shape a more equitable, resilient, and transparent digital future.

person.name: Cornelis Stuurman
person.bio: I am a Cloud Native/DevOps engineer that likes diving into bigger problems and finding out of the box solutions. I love learning about new tools and concepts in the Cloud Native Landscape.

person.name: Davey Van Huffelen
person.bio: Infrastructure Architect specialized in Kubernetes and DevOps tooling. Davey is focused on creating scalable and secure infrastructure, with a strong focus on automation. He is passionate about all things Kubernetes and adjacent projects like ArgoCD, Prometheus and more.

person.name: Eduardo Munari

person.name: Hemanth Ande

He has hands-on work with tools like Istio, Cilium, Falco, and Trivy.

person.name: Icaro Rodrigues

My specialties are automation and observability; I leverage CNCF tooling such as KEDA, ArgoCD, Prometheus, and Istio to architect and orchestrate scalable infrastructure solutions across AWS and Oracle Cloud. My journey as a Kubestronaut validates my deep Kubernetes knowledge and fuels my mission to help teams understand not just the how, but the crucial why behind modern cloud-native practices.

person.name: Jonas Dale Fredriksen

person.name: Tawei Chen
person.bio: I am an engineer with a deep passion for cloud-native technologies and am honored to have recently been awarded the Kubestronaut title by the CNCF.  My love for open source is rooted in its spirit of collaboration and innovation, and I am dedicated to leveraging Kubernetes technologies to solve complex challenges and build resilient, scalable modern applications.  This honor marks a new beginning, and I am eager to engage more deeply with the community, learn from peers worldwide, and contribute to this thriving ecosystem.

person.name: Mads Urang Nilsen
person.bio: I'mAs a trained Materials Scientist turned cloud-native enthusiast, I’m now focused on leveraging code to optimize workflows and drive innovation. Currently, I serve as a Senior Engineer at Sopra Steria, where I apply my expertise to tackle complex challenges and streamline processes. My passion lies in creating efficient, scalable solutions through code!

person.name: Nelson Hilario Filipe
person.bio: As a DevSecOps Engineer with a strong passion for shift-left security, I embarked on the Kubestronaut program to deepen my expertise in securing Kubernetes environments and workloads. I'm dedicated to implementing security measures throughout the entire development lifecycle, from development to deployment, to enable secure operations and maintain a strong security posture for cloud-native applications.

person.name: Roman Hüsler

My blog, opensight.ch, shares insights on automation, cloud-native apps, and scripting, tackling issues that resonate with the tech community. Connect with me on LinkedIn for engaging discussions and to explore my journey further!

person.name: Sreenivas Chokkarapu

I focus on optimizing infrastructure, implementing security best practices, and automating deployments to improve system reliability. I stay up to date with the latest CNCF trends through meetups and technical content.

person.name: Tan Kar Chun
person.bio: I'm a DevOps engineer who loves Kubernetes, Docker, Cloud, and Architecture. Passion means everything to me, as part of my passion and motivation, I love to inspire people and my ambition is to develop a new technology that can revolutionize the world.

person.name: Alolita Sharma
person.bio:
Alolita is an OpenTelemetry GC member, CNCF Observability TAG co-chair and CNCF Governing Board alternate board member from Apple. She leads Apple’s AIML observability practice and contributes to open source and open standards in OpenTelemetry, Unicode, W3C and the Observability TAG.

As a founding member of the CNCF’s End User TAB, Alolita has facilitated participation and contributions by CNCF end user members. She has also promoted end user priorities to the CNCF TOC and Governing Board. Within her rich track record, Alolita has demonstrated a deep commitment to CNCF's goals and initiatives. Alolita brings a wealth of experience from her board leadership roles in the industry. For example, she has served on the boards of the OSI, SFLC.in and Unicode Consortium. Alolita has also provided strong leadership for observability and search engineering at AWS and has managed engineering teams at IBM, PayPal, Twitter, and Wikipedia.

person.name: Namig Azimli
person.bio: I am a DevOps engineer with over 2 years of dedicated professional experience in the field. My expertise extends to system automation, where I adeptly leverage prominent DevOps tools, including but not limited to Ansible, Gitlab CI, Docker, and Kubernetes.

person.name: Victor Caceres Chian
person.bio: Data Engineer focused on building scalable data platforms, ETL pipelines, and CI/CD workflows in cloud-native environments. Interested in Kubernetes and platform engineering, with a continuous learning mindset.

person.name: Atchiba Hichem
person.bio: I am a Customer Technical Manager at Ericsson with over 20 years of experience in the telecommunications industry. Throughout my career, I have led complex technical engagements, supporting service providers in delivering reliable and scalable network solutions. My expertise spans telecom architecture, network transformation, and customer-focused technical strategy.

person.name: Jérôme Masson

My journey into cloud-native technologies started in 2017 with Docker and Kubernetes, and has since evolved to include open-source software within the CNCF Landscape. As a consultant with expertise in platform engineering projects, I am passionate about sharing my knowledge at conferences such as Devoxx FR, KCD France, and DevFest Nantes.

person.name: Sean Harrington

I am passionate about demystifying complex systems and empowering development teams to operate their services with confidence.

person.name: Sven Pimmer
person.bio: I am a System Engineer with a deep passion for Kubernetes, containerization, and cloud-native technologies. With extensive experience in installing and maintaining Kubernetes clusters, including in on-premise environments, I thrive on optimizing and bringing cloud-native applications everywhere.

person.name: Giorgi Godishvili

With hands-on experience in deploying and managing scalable, resilient infrastructure, I work to streamline CI/CD pipelines, improve operational efficiency, and help teams embrace DevOps best practices.

person.name: Ibrahim Tayara

person.name: Jae Choi

This allows me to stay current and pragmatic when making technical decisions.

He is also the founder of Cloud Native Community China, where he has worked extensively to bridge open source communities, developers, and enterprises.In recent years, Jimmy’s work has shifted deeper into infrastructure fundamentals, particularly around GPU sharing, scheduling, isolation, utilization, and cost control. He advocates treating compute — especially GPU — as a first-class, governable infrastructure resource, and actively contributes to building scalable, open ecosystems around this vision.Jimmy is also an author and speaker, sharing insights on the intersection of cloud native technologies and AI infrastructure through writing, talks, and community initiatives.</p

person.name: Cristher Castro
person.bio: Cristher Castro, co-founder and COO of Cuemby, is a visionary leader and operational dynamo who drives community building in the Latam tech scene. Over the last 3 years, she advocated for the adoption of open-source technologies within the Spanish-speaking community, creating and supporting tech meetups and events in the region. She co-created, along with her team, the first Kubernetes Community Days in Colombia. This event paved the way for the expansion of Cloud Native Groups throughout the region. Cristher's foresight and operational expertise have been fundamental to the success of both Cuemby and the Cloud Native Latam Group. Recognizing the region's need for in-depth knowledge of cloud-native applications, Cristher championed the LFS250 (Linux Foundation Course) and administered the KCNA certifications donated by CNCF to the region. These two initiatives heightened awareness of cloud-native technologies and certifications, fortifying local proficiency in open-source solutions. She continues to promote and advocate for the recognition of the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) in the region, addressing both business and community aspects. Cristher's leadership and operational prowess exemplify her unwavering commitment to fostering a thriving Latam tech community. https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-latin-america/ https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-medellin/ https://www.cncf.io/reports/kcd-colombia-2023/ https://www.cuemby.com/about-us https://www.linkedin.com/company/fundaci%C3%B3n-hispana-de-cloud-native/?viewAsMember=true https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-colombia/ https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/kcd-colombia/?viewAsMember=truehttps://www.linkedin.com/showcase/kcd-colombiaWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: Becoming a CNCF ambassador empowers me to further my dedication to community growth in the LATAM region. I deeply believe that the region's success hinges on the symbiotic collaboration of professionals, students, and companies. It's this harmony that forms the core of my mission: to cultivate a thriving community ecosystem centered around open-source and cloud-native technologies. Through this role, I am positioned to amplify awareness about these technologies and the transformative opportunities they introduce. My operational and logistics expertise is set to facilitate more meetups and events, thus kindling the formation of new communities. Collaborating with fellow leaders and ambassadors provides a unique platform to unite our efforts, ensuring we collectively advance our shared vision.

He also enjoys writing technical blog posts and wrote 10+ blog posts and all of which are published on important official websites including Kyverno, CD Foundation, Flux, Sigstore, etc. • https://cd.foundation/blog/2022/10/18/tekton-chains/ • https://fluxcd.io/blog/2022/10/prove-the-authenticity-of-oci-artifacts/ • https://fluxcd.io/blog/2022/08/manage-kyverno-policies-as-ocirepositories/ • https://fluxcd.io/blog/2022/11/verify-the-integrity-of-the-helm-charts-stored-as-oci-artifacts-before-reconciling-them-with-flux/ • https://kubesimplify.com/the-secret-gems-behind-building-container-images-enter-buildkit-and-docker-buildx • https://blog.kubesimplify.com/getting-started-with-ko-a-fast-container-image-builder-for-your-go-applications • https://nirmata.com/securing_base_images/ • https://blog.sigstore.dev/how-to-verify-container-images-with-kyverno-using-kms-cosign-and-workload-identity-1e07d2b85061Why I am excited to be an ambassador: First of all, I'm the first person from Turkey who has been selected as a CNCF Ambassador which is super great for me because I have the energy to spread all my knowledge with other people and I do really like to collaborate with people by organizing in-person, virtual meetups. person.name: Konrad Rzentarzewski
person.bio: I was there when the config burned. In the age before observability, chaos reigned. The servers howled, and nothing scaled but entropy. Then kube cattle were born — simple, nameless beasts born of children’s games. But they multiplied. And their hunger grew vast. Years passed. The sun revolved the Earth. Then the Earth revolved the Cloud. I grew tired of debugging in darkness. I longed for light. And logs. Preferably structured. That’s when I began preaching — not loudly, just… declaratively. One apply at a time. I walked among DevOps tribes, where bash begat shell, and shell begat pain. To them I brought ConfigMaps, and Secrets hidden in plain sight. Then came the mysterious YAML — a language too holy to lint — inscribed deep within the API’s own heart. Through toil and merge, the infrastructure became manifest. Pods floated on the waters. Deployments begat replicas. Helm bore Charts. Git begat Ops. What was once dev spun to prod. And every service had a name — and every name resolved. Unless external DNS was involved. And the Preachers said: \"Behold, our logs streameth clean.\" Thus began the age of the declarative. We once dwelled in darkness. Now, we scale planetary in full light.

Why he is motivated: "I love seeing the spark of satisfaction in people's eyes when I have helped them learn something or see things from a different perspective."

I am also a committer on a number of IoT related projects and interested in observability and metrics – as a side project I look at how Formula 1 telemetry can be analysed with the technologies I work with every day. I am based in Napoli, where I am the lead organiser of the local CNCF Meetup.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I feel that I am one of the lucky people who love their job because it's also their passion. Taking this passion to the community by sharing my knowledge about open source technologies is something which excites me. To be a CNCF Ambassador takes this love to the next level, being even more engaged within the cloud native space.

person.name: Roby Chen
person.bio: I am honored to continue serving in this role. Over the past few years, My company DaoCloud has made continuous contributions to the open-source community. Our contributions to Kubernetes, a core cloud-native project, have ranked among the top three globally over the past year. In 2022, we will also take on a contributing seat on Istio's steering committee. We have independently open-sourced nine projects, including Clusterpedia, Merbridge and Piraeus which have successfully entered CNCF Sandbox incubation. DaoCloud continues to contribute significantly towards cloud-native technology and improve CNCF's technical ecosystem.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Firstly, I hope that cloud-native technology will remain highly connected to China, and that the integration of the technology world will not be affected by geography, politics, international conflicts and other factors. Secondly, I hope that cloud-native technology can break through the barriers between technology and users, and that more end-user voices can be fed back to CNCF. Thirdly, I hope to promote the building of cloud-native technology talent and further improve the cloud-native education system, so that CNCF will become a sustainable organization.

I hope to inspire more people to join the cloud native journey and make a positive impact on the world.

He is one of the maintainers of the CNCF Cloud Native Glossary which aims to define cloud native concepts in clear and simple language, and he is contributing to Kubernetes by leading the Korean localization team in Kubernetes SIG-Docs and maintaining localization in Kubernetes/website. Furthermore, he is one of the maintainers of Cloud-Barista, a multi-cloud management platform. As an open source advocate, Seokho has served as a speaker at various cloud native events, including Cloud Native Community Groups and Kubernetes Forum in South Korea, and he is actively involved in mentoring open source contribution programs in South Korea.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am thrilled to have become a CNCF Ambassador and excited to continue contributing to cloud-native technology. I have been working on reducing language barriers in cloud-native technologies by localizing technical documents in Kubernetes, and maintaining the CNCF Cloud Native Glossary project to make cloud-native concepts accessible to anyone, regardless of their technical background. Through my community activities, I have had the pleasure of meeting many talented and friendly contributors, and I look forward to continue developing these relationships. I believe that being a CNCF ambassador is an extraordinary opportunity for me to spread cloud-native technologies even further. As a CNCF Ambassador, I am aiming to be a reliable source of information for the Korean community by sharing the latest technology information and community trends in a timely manner, and I hope to inspire Korean experts to get involved in global activities.

Six years ago, Harsha didn’t know the meaning of DevOps or software engineering—just a kid with dreams and an electronics background who came to the USA to pursue a Master’s degree in Computer Systems Engineering at Northeastern University. Guided by excellent mentors and faculty, he discovered his passion for infrastructure and cloud-native technologies, beginning his career at a small startup where he learned the fundamentals of DevOps practices. Today, he manages enterprise-level infrastructure, leveraging Docker, Kubernetes, and Terraform to orchestrate complex systems, and is now advancing his expertise through the Kubestronauts program to deepen his mastery of Kubernetes and contribute meaningfully to the cloud-native community. Harsha is eager to connect with fellow engineers, community members, and innovators to share experiences, learn from diverse perspectives, and grow together in the DevOps and Kubernetes ecosystem.

person.name: Doddy Kristianto
person.bio: I have worked in a telecommunications company for almost 19 years at a leading digital telecommunications company in Indonesia. I started my journey in the field of virtualization and cloud technology from 2015 until now. Physical to Virtual (P2V) and Virtual to Virtual (V2V) are the first experiences in virtualization projects that know the basics of cloud computing.  I started planning, assessing and building cloud computing in my company since the end of 2019 by conducting several cloud technology assessment tenders and selecting hyperscalers (cloud provider) according to my company's use cases. our challenge is how we maximize the benefits of cloud computing by migrating monolithic applications to microservices from on-premises to cloud using hyperscaler, with microservices we will have agile delivery to support rapid Go to Market (GTM) from a business point of view, we can support multiple deployments or sprint releases according to the business requirements program every week, it is much easier to maintain our applications / services independently and much more. Therefore, we realize the need to have microservices knowledge by understanding in-depth knowledge of the Kubernetes Platform to support this so that we will have an agile environment that can support our business growth and provide added value and competitive advantage for digital transformation in our company. I started learning Kubernetes from 2022 until now to support all kinds of applications/services migrating to microservices in the cloud. by fully understanding this knowledge we can maximize the value of kubernetes to support our business in our company.

His Sigma rules were referenced by SANS ICS, while his co-authored open-source project (ATC RE&CT) was featured in the SANS MGT551 course. His dedication to staying at the forefront of industry trends and advancements has earned him a reputation as a knowledgeable and effective cybersecurity expert. His published paper, which received the Best Paper Award at IEEE SOLI 2021, can be accessed at: "ASIST: Automated Strategic Partners Ranking and Recommendation System Using NLP and Security Intelligence," 2021 IEEE International Conference on Service Operations and Logistics, and Informatics (SOLI), 2021 (https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9672369).

person.name: Davit Harutyunyan

Alongside his academic pursuits, he worked as a Research Engineer in the Smart Networks and Services (SENSE) group at the FBK Research Institute in Italy from 2015 to 2020, and since 2021, he has been contributing as a Research Engineer in Corporate Research at Robert Bosch GmbH in Germany.  His primary research interests focus on advancing 5G/6G mobile networks, Open RAN, Network Function Virtualization (NFV), network automation, management, and orchestration. He has extensive experience in edge and cloud computing, particularly in leveraging lightweight virtualization technologies such as containers and their orchestration. He is also proficient in using DevOps tools and public cloud services for efficient and scalable infrastructure deployment.

person.name: Shu Muto
person.bio: Shu Muto is a Principal Software Engineer at NEC Solution Innovators, Ltd., a company within the NEC group.He serves as one of the maintainers for Kubernetes Dashboard and holds the position of chair for SIG UI.Since 2019, Shu has been organizing Kubernetes Upstream Training sessions in Japan, dedicated to training both contributors and mentors, and to promote the culture of open source.Kubernetes Upstream Training in JapanKubernetes Upstream Training in remoteThe following presentations were given in relation to Kubernetes Upstream Training:[KCSNA2019] How to Hold Upstream Training in Your Country and Get New Contributors! - Shu Muto, Kenichi Oomichi, Akihito Inoh[KCSNA2022] How Kubernetes upstream training has been held in Japan for four years, getting over the COVID-19 situation - Shu Muto, Xie Ziyi[KubeDay Japan 2024] Keynote: Empowering Japanese Contributors: Kubernetes Upstream Training - Shu Muto, Ziyi XieIn addition, he serves as an organizer for the CNCF chapter in Japan, CNCF Cloud Native Community Japan, which was launched in November 2023.He hopes to spread the culture of development within the open source community more than ever before and promote collaboration among developers around the world.

He currently leads the Cloud and Architecture domain at SAS, for the South West & East Europe region. He holds a BA in Archaeology from the University of Lisbon, where he is an MA student and where he also took Physics.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I see being a CNCF Ambassador as a great opportunity to engage with others both in the technical aspects of cloud-native computing and in the many ways that those interested can become active members of the community. Cloud-native technology has largely been developed through open collaboration, and being an Ambassador is taking a step towards more visible participation in building the community - and learning from it.
